title: Federal Reserve Board announces termination of enforcement actions with Crédit Agricole S.A. and Crédit Agricole Corporate and Investment Bank, Mega International Commercial Bank Co., Ltd, and the Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.
summary: The Federal Reserve Board announced the termination of three Cease and Desist Orders against Crédit Agricole S.A. and its investment bank subsidiary (October 19, 2015 order), Mega International Commercial Bank Co., Ltd. and its US branches (January 17, 2018 order), and The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (May 1, 2018 order), all effective March 25, 2026.
key_points: Crédit Agricole S.A. and Crédit Agricole Corporate and Investment Bank: Cease and Desist Order dated October 19, 2015, terminated March 25, 2026
Mega International Commercial Bank Co., Ltd. (Taipei, Taiwan) with New York, Chicago, and Silicon Valley branches: Cease and Desist Order dated January 17, 2018, terminated March 25, 2026
The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (New York): Cease and Desist Order dated May 1, 2018, terminated March 25, 2026
All three enforcement actions were terminated on the same date: March 25, 2026
Announcement made by the Federal Reserve Board on April 9, 2026
why_it_matters: This is a routine regulatory housekeeping item marking the resolution of prior enforcement actions. Terminated Cease and Desist Orders indicate that the banks addressed the compliance deficiencies that prompted the original actions. However, this does not represent a new regulatory development or policy signal that would influence market expectations about interest rates, economic growth, or systemic risk.
market_structure_context: This press release concerns the termination of prior enforcement actions against specific financial institutions. It does not discuss market plumbing, payment systems, clearing infrastructure, or operational framework changes.
